executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 102 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims in the last 48 hours across all economic sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart with confirmed database leakage, Peterborough Public Health, and the Municipality of North Perth. The attack wave involves at least 25 distinct ransomware groups, with Qilin (21 victims), Clop (14 victims), Play (10 victims), and SafePay (10 victims) leading the assault. Critical infrastructure sectors hit include healthcare, education, public sector services, and financial services, indicating a coordinated or opportunistic mass exploitation event requiring immediate incident response across Canadian industry.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Waterloo Information Systems, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Workers Health & Safety Centre, Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Questica, MG CPA, CST Coal, Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Pre-Con Builders, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, A Lococo Wholesale, Watermark Beach Resort

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a severe ransomware crisis with 102 confirmed victims across all sectors reported in the last 48 hours. Critical incidents include attacks on healthcare facilities (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services, Bison Family Medical), educational institutions (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, Teccart), public sector organizations (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, Thunder Bay Counselling, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed database leakage, Morison Insurance Brokers). No CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross automotive retail chain and Kids & Company childcare services were also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a confirmed database leak by the Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers, multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant), and SMT Lawyers were also compromised. No attacks on major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) were identified.

report_2w.executive_summary: Two critical vulnerabilities are under active exploitation right now: Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616) and Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway (CVE-2026-3055), both flagged by the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security. A Windows zero-day privilege escalation exploit called BlueHammer was publicly released by a disgruntled researcher, with no patch currently available. The China-linked group Storm-1175 is exploiting zero-day flaws to rapidly deploy Medusa ransomware, while Wynn Resorts disclosed that 21,000 employees were affected by a ShinyHunters breach. German police have unmasked the suspected leader of the REvil ransomware gang.

items.description: Improper access control vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS enabling remote code execution
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: Storm-1175 (China-linked)
items.targets: Organizations globally with web-facing assets; deploying Medusa ransomware
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), rapid exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities within hours of disclosure

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Wynn Resorts (21,000 employees affected); Hims & Hers customer data breach
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: REvil (GandCrab)
items.targets: Historical ransomware operations; suspected leader unmasked by German police
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Iran-linked APT (unnamed)
items.targets: 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 organizations; Iranian workers recruited by North Korea for IT job fraud
items.ttps: T1110.003 (Password Spraying), T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Medusa Ransomware (linked to Storm-1175)
items.targets: Organizations with exploitable web-facing vulnerabilities
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), rapid time-to-ransom deployment

items.name: Silent Ransom Group
items.targets: Jones Day law firm (limited breach via phishing)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Organizations using EDR tools; uses vulnerable drivers to disable 300+ EDR solutions
items.ttps: T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Warlock Ransomware
items.targets: Organizations using EDR tools; uses vulnerable drivers to disable 300+ EDR solutions
items.ttps: T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: TA416 (China-aligned)
items.targets: European government entities (espionage campaigns resumed since mid-2025)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol)

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: Broad targeting for financial gain and espionage; recruiting Iranian workers for IT job fraud
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1204 (User Execution), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued two critical alerts this period: CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS (actively exploited) and CVE-2026-3055 impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway. Both are high-priority patching targets for Canadian organizations. No specific incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
